records.chapter: Reward of reciting Surah Qadr while cutting a cloth for new dress
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Muhammad from his father from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. al-Hussain from Muhammad b. Umar al-Sarad from the one that narrated to him from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “A person should recite Surah Qadr thirty-six times while cutting a new cloth. When he reaches Tanazzalul malaa-ikatu, he should sprinkle a little water on the cloth. He should then offer a two rakat Salaat and recite this supplication Alh’amdulillaahilladhee razaqanee maa atajammalu bihi finnaasi wa uwaaree bihi a’uratee wa us’allee feehi lirabbee wa ah’madullaah. Before this cloth gets old, there would be abundance in livelihood.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عُمَرَ السَّرَّادِ عَمَّنْ أَخْبَرَهُ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَطَعَ ثَوْباً جَدِیداً وَ قَرَأَ إِنَّا أَنْزَلْناهُ فِی لَیْلَهِ الْقَدْرِ سِتّاً وَ ثَلَاثِینَ مَرَّهً فَإِذَا بَلَغَ تَنَزَّلُ الْمَلائِکَهُ أَخْرَجَ شَیْئاً مِنَ الْمَاءِ وَ رَشَّ عَلَی الثَّوْبِ رَشّاً خَفِیفاً ثُمَّ صَلَّی رَکْعَتَیْنِ وَ دَعَا رَبَّهُ وَ قَالَ فِی دُعَائِهِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِی رَزَقَنِی مَا أَتَجَمَّلُ بِهِ فِی النَّاسِ وَ أُوَارِی بِهِ عَوْرَتِی وَ أُصَلِّی فِیهِ لِرَبِّی وَ حَمِدَ اللَّهَ لَمْ یَزَلْ یَأْکُلُ فِی سَعَهٍ حَتَّی یَبْلَی ذَلِکَ الثَّوْبُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Muhammad from his father from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. al-Hussain from Muhammad b. Umar al-Sarad from the one that narrated to him from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “A person should recite Surah Qadr thirty-six times while cutting a new cloth. When he reaches Tanazzalul malaa-ikatu, he should sprinkle a little water on the cloth. He should then offer a two rakat Salaat and recite this supplication Alh’amdulillaahilladhee razaqanee maa atajammalu bihi finnaasi wa uwaaree bihi a’uratee wa us’allee feehi lirabbee wa ah’madullaah. Before this cloth gets old, there would be abundance in livelihood.”

records.chapter: Reward of praising Allah much while looking in the mirror
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Harun b. Muslim from Mas’ada b. Sadaqah from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his forefathers (a.s.) that The Prophet (s.a.w.s.) who said: “The Almighty Allah has decreed Paradise for those youths who look at the mirror and praise Allah much.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مَسْعَدَهَ بْنِ صَدَقَهَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع أَنَّ النَّبِیَّ ص قَالَ: إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ أَوْجَبَ الْجَنَّهَ لِشَابٍّ کَانَ یُکْثِرُ النَّظَرَ فِی الْمِرْآهِ فَیُکْثِرُ حَمْدَ اللَّهِ عَلَی ذَلِکَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Harun b. Muslim from Mas’ada b. Sadaqah from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his forefathers (a.s.) that
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Prophet (s.a.w.s.) who said: “The Almighty Allah has decreed Paradise for those youths who look at the mirror and praise Allah much.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting the following supplication upon seeing a Jew, Christian or a Magian
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Harun b. Muslim from Mas’ada b. Sadaqah from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father from his forefathers (a.s.) that The Prophet (s.a.w.s.) who said: “One who recites Alh’amdulillaahilladhee faz”z”alnee a’laika bil islaami deenanw wa bi qur-aani kitaabanw wa bimuh’ammadin nabeeyanw wa bi-a’liyyin imaamanw wa bil mu-mineena ikhwaananw wabil ka’bati qiblah Allah won’t gather that person along with those people.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مَسْعَدَهَ بْنِ صَدَقَهَ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع أَنَّ النَّبِیَّ ص قَالَ: مَنْ رَأَی یَهُودِیّاً أَوْ نَصْرَانِیّاً أَوْ مَجُوسِیّاً أَوْ وَاحِداً عَلَی غَیْرِ مِلَّهِ الْإِسْلَامِ فَقَالَ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِی فَضَّلَنِی عَلَیْکَ بِالْإِسْلَامِ دِیناً وَ بِالْقُرْآنِ کِتَاباً وَ بِمُحَمَّدٍ نَبِیّاً وَ بِعَلِیٍّ إِمَاماً وَ بِالْمُؤْمِنِینَ إِخْوَاناً وَ بِالْکَعْبَهِ قِبْلَهً لَمْ یَجْمَعِ اللَّهُ بَیْنَهُ وَ بَیْنَهُ فِی النَّارِ أَبَداً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Harun b. Muslim from Mas’ada b. Sadaqah from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his father from his forefathers (a.s.) that
records.thaqalaynMatn: The Prophet (s.a.w.s.) who said: “One who recites Alh’amdulillaahilladhee faz”z”alnee a’laika bil islaami deenanw wa bi qur-aani kitaabanw wa bimuh’ammadin nabeeyanw wa bi-a’liyyin imaamanw wa bil mu-mineena ikhwaananw wabil ka’bati qiblah Allah won’t gather that person along with those people.”

records.chapter: Reward of complete ablution, Umrah, payment of Zakat…
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Musa Kadhim (a.s.) narrates from his father, Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who  performs  complete  ablution,  offers  Salaat  regularly, pays Zakat of his wealth regularly, controls his anger and tongue, repents for his sins and seeks goodness of the Ahle Bayt (a.s.) of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) then all the duties of his  faith  are  fulfilled  and  the  doors  of  Paradise  would  be opened for him.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی الْعَمْرَکِیُّ عَنِ الْبُوفَکِیِّ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَخِیهِ مُوسَی بْنِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ أَسْبَغَ وُضُوءَهُ وَ أَحْسَنَ صَلَاتَهُ وَ أَدَّی زَکَاهَ مَالِهِ وَ کَفَّ غَضَبَهُ وَ سَجَنَ لِسَانَهُ وَ اسْتَغْفَرَ لِذَنْبِهِ وَ أَدَّی النَّصِیحَهَ لِأَهْلِ بَیْتِ نَبِیِّهِ ص فَقَدِ اسْتَکْمَلَ حَقَائِقَ الْإِیمَانِ وَ أَبْوَابُ الْجَنَّهِ مَفْتُوحَهٌ لَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam Musa Kadhim (a.s.) narrates from his father, Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who  performs  complete  ablution,  offers  Salaat  regularly, pays Zakat of his wealth regularly, controls his anger and tongue, repents for his sins and seeks goodness of the Ahle Bayt (a.s.) of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) then all the duties of his  faith  are  fulfilled  and  the  doors  of  Paradise  would  be opened for him.”

records.chapter: Reward of reciting Raz”eetubillaahi rabbanw…
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  The  Holy  Prophet  (s.a.w.s.)  says,  “One  who  recites Raz”eetubillaahi   rabbanw   wa   bil   islaami   deenanw   wa bimuh’ammadir  rasoolanw wa bi-ahli baytihi awliyaa –  he has right that Allah becomes pleased with him on the Day of Judgment.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ قَالَ رَضِیتُ بِاللَّهِ رَبّاً وَ بِالْإِسْلَامِ دِیناً وَ بِمُحَمَّدٍ رَسُولًا وَ بِأَهْلِ بَیْتِهِ أَوْلِیَاءَ کَانَ حَقّاً عَلَی اللَّهِ أَنْ یُرْضِیَهُ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ.

records.chapter: Reward of supplicating day and night
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. And from the previous chain who said: “Supplicate day and night; for supplication is the weapon of a believer.”
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ قَالَ: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص أَ لَا أَدُلُّکُمْ عَلَی سِلَاحٍ یُنْجِیکُمْ عَنْ عَدُوِّکُمْ وَ یُدِرُّ رِزْقَکُمْ قَالُوا نَعَمْ قَالَ تَدْعُونَ بِاللَّیْلِ وَ النَّهَارِ فَإِنَّ سِلَاحَ الْمُؤْمِنِ الدُّعَاءُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. And from the previous chain who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Supplicate day and night; for supplication is the weapon of a believer.”

records.chapter: Reward of going to a mosque
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. al-Hussain b. Abi al-Khattab from Safwan b. Yahya from Kulayb al-Saydawi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Know that those who visit mosque in darkness will get bright eyesight and glad-tidings on the Day of Judgment.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ أَبِی الْخَطَّابِ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ بْنِ یَحْیَی عَنْ کُلَیْبٍ الصَّیْدَاوِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَکْتُوبٌ فِی التَّوْرَاهِ أَنَّ بُیُوتِی فِی الْأَرْضِ الْمَسَاجِدُ فَطُوبَی لِعَبْدٍ تَطَهَّرَ فِی بَیْتِهِ ثُمَّ زَارَنِی فِی بَیْتِی أَلَا إِنَّ عَلَی الْمَزُورِ کَرَامَهَ الزَّائِرِ وَ فِی حَدِیثٍ آخَرَ أَلَا بَشِّرِ الْمَشَّاءِینَ فِی الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَی الْمَسَاجِدِ بِالنُّورِ السَّاطِعِ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. al-Hassan al-Saffar from Muhammad b. al-Hussain b. Abi al-Khattab from Safwan b. Yahya from Kulayb al-Saydawi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Know that those who visit mosque in darkness will get bright eyesight and glad-tidings on the Day of Judgment.”

records.chapter: Reward of visiting mosque often
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Amirul Momineen (a.s.) says, “Those who visit mosque often would get the benefit of (at least) one of these eight things  –  benefiting  from  a  brother  in  the  way  of Allah or gaining  extra  knowledge,  or  appearance  of  a  strong  sign, gaining  that  mercy  which  was  awaited,  benefiting  from conversation which saves one from destruction, or hearing about     an act of guidance, or keeping away from sins out of fear or keeping away from sins out of shame.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْحَمِیدِ عَنْ سَعْدٍ الْإِسْکَافِیِّ عَنْ زِیَادِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنْ أَبِی الْجَارُودِ عَنِ الْأَصْبَغِ بْنِ نُبَاتَهَ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ أَبِی طَالِبٍ ع قَالَ کَانَ یَقُولُ مَنِ اخْتَلَفَ إِلَی الْمَسَاجِدِ أَصَابَ إِحْدَی الثَّمَانِ أَخاً مُسْتَفَاداً فِی اللَّهِ أَوْ عِلْماً مُسْتَطْرَفاً أَوْ آیَهً مُحْکَمَهً أَوْ رَحْمَهً مُنْتَظَرَهً أَوْ کَلِمَهً تَرْدَعُهُ عَنْ رَدًی أَوْ یَسْمَعُ کَلِمَهً تَدُلُّهُ عَلَی هُدًی أَوْ یَتْرُکُ ذَنْباً خَشْیَهً أَوْ حَیَاءً.

records.chapter: Reward of going to a mosque on foot
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Ya’la b. Hamzah from al-Hajjal from Ali b. al-Hakam from Muhammad b. Harun from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who goes to mosque on foot, everything will glorify Allah for him from the place where he steps on be it wet or dry till the seventh heaven.
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ یَحْیَی الْعَطَّارُ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ یَعْلَی بْنِ حَمْزَهَ عَنِ الْحَجَّالِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحَکَمِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ هَارُونَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ مَشَی إِلَی الْمَسَاجِدِ لَمْ یَضَعْ رِجْلَهُ عَلَی رَطْبٍ وَ لَا یَابِسٍ إِلَّا سَبَّحَتْ لَهُ الْأَرْضُ إِلَی الْأَرَضِینَ السَّابِعَهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh who said it was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Yahya al-’Attar from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Ya’la b. Hamzah from al-Hajjal from Ali b. al-Hakam from Muhammad b. Harun from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who goes to mosque on foot, everything will glorify Allah for him from the place where he steps on be it wet or dry till the seventh heaven.

records.chapter: Reward of Quranic conversation and making mosque one’s home
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “One who makes Quran his conversation and mosque his home Allah will make a house for him in Paradise.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی حَمْزَهُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدٍ الْعَلَوِیُّ ره قَالَ أَخْبَرَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنِ النَّوْفَلِیِّ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ کَانَ الْقُرْآنُ حَدِیثَهُ وَ الْمَسْجِدُ بَیْتَهُ بَنَی اللَّهُ لَهُ بَیْتاً فِی الْجَنَّهِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who makes Quran his conversation and mosque his home Allah will make a house for him in Paradise.”

records.chapter: Reward of going to mosque after performing ablution
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Muhammad b. al-Hussain from Safwan from Kulayb al-Saydawi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “It is written in the Old Testament: ‘My houses on earth are mosques. A slave of Mine who performs ablution in his house and come to My house for meeting Me shall have Tooba (a good fortune). Know that it is necessary for the host to honor his guest.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَبْدُ اللَّهِ بْنُ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ عَنْ صَفْوَانَ عَنْ کُلَیْبٍ الصَّیْدَاوِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَکْتُوبٌ فِی التَّوْرَاهِ أَنَّ بُیُوتِی فِی الْأَرْضِ الْمَسَاجِدُ فَطُوبَی لِمَنْ تَطَهَّرَ فِی بَیْتِهِ ثُمَّ زَارَنِی وَ حَقٌّ عَلَی الْمَزُورِ أَنْ یُکْرِمَ الزَّائِرَ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Abdillah b. Ja’far from Muhammad b. al-Hussain from Safwan from Kulayb al-Saydawi from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “It is written in the Old Testament: ‘My houses on earth are mosques. A slave of Mine who performs ablution in his house and come to My house for meeting Me shall have Tooba (a good fortune). Know that it is necessary for the host to honor his guest.”

records.chapter: Reward of going to mosque after performing ablution
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.  The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) says, “Know that Allah says, ‘My houses on the earth are mosques. As stars provide light to the people on earth mosques do to the people on heaven. Know that those people have Tooba (good fortune) whose houses are mosques. Also know that a slave of Mine who performs ablution in his house and come to My house for meeting Me shall have Tooba (good fortune). Know that it is necessary for the host to honor his guest. Know that there are  glad tidings of bright light on the Day of Judgment for one who visits the mosque in darkness.
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ جَبَلَهَ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ سُلَیْمَانَ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص قَالَ اللَّهُ تَبَارَکَ وَ تَعَالَی أَلَا إِنَّ بُیُوتِی فِی الْأَرْضِ الْمَسَاجِدُ تُضِی ءُ لِأَهْلِ السَّمَاءِ کَمَا تُضِی ءُ النُّجُومُ لِأَهْلِ الْأَرْضِ أَلَا طُوبَی لِمَنْ کَانَتِ الْمَسَاجِدُ بُیُوتَهُ أَلَا طُوبَی لِعَبْدٍ تَوَضَّأَ فِی بَیْتِهِ ثُمَّ زَارَنِی فِی بَیْتِی أَلَا إِنَّ عَلَی الْمَزُورِ کَرَامَهَ الزَّائِرِ أَلَا بَشِّرِ الْمَشَّاءِینَ فِی الظُّلُمَاتِ إِلَی الْمَسَاجِدِ بِالنُّورِ السَّاطِعِ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ.

records.chapter: Reward of going to mosque after performing ablution
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3.  Amirul Momineen (a.s.) says, “Whenever people of the earth  commit  sins  or  get  involved  in  wrong  deeds,  the Almighty Allah intends to send down His chastisement on the  people  of  the  world.  However  when  He  sees  elderly people praying and young children learning Quran, He feels pity on them and does not send down His chastisement.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ هِشَامٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ إِسْمَاعِیلَ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحَکَمِ عَنْ سَیْفِ بْنِ عَمِیرَهَ عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ ظَرِیفٍ عَنِ الْأَصْبَغِ بْنِ نُبَاتَهَ قَالَ قَالَ أَمِیرُ الْمُؤْمِنِینَ ع إِنَّ اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ لَیَهُمُّ بِعَذَابِ أَهْلِ الْأَرْضِ جَمِیعاً لَا یُحَاشِی مِنْهُمْ أَحَداً إِذَا عَمِلُوا بِالْمَعَاصِی وَ اجْتَرَحُوا السَّیِّئَاتِ فَإِذَا نَظَرَ إِلَی الشِّیبِ نَاقِلِی أَقْدَامِهِمْ إِلَی الصَّلَاهِ وَ الْوِلْدَانِ یَتَعَلَّمُونَ الْقُرْآنَ رَحِمَهُمْ فَأَخَّرَ ذَلِکَ عَنْهُمْ.

records.chapter: Reward of offering five times’ prayers on prime time
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says, “O Abaan! One who offers obligatory prayers five times and that too on prime time, he will go to Allah with an assurance on the Day of Judgment and Allah would make him enter Paradise. And for a person who did not offer prayers on time, Allah has choice whether to punish him or forgive him.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ الْحَجَّاجِ عَنْ أَبَانِ بْنِ تَغْلِبَ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع یَا أَبَانُ هَذِهِ الصَّلَوَاتُ الْخَمْسُ الْمَفْرُوضَاتُ مَنْ أَقَامَهُنَّ وَ حَافَظَ عَلَی مَوَاقِیتِهِنَّ لَقِیَ اللَّهَ یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ وَ لَهُ عِنْدَهُ عَهْدٌ یُدْخِلُهُ بِهِ الْجَنَّهَ وَ مَنْ لَمْ یُصَلِّهِنَّ لِمَوَاقِیتِهِنَّ فَذَلِکَ إِلَیْهِ إِنْ شَاءَ غَفَرَ لَهُ وَ إِنْ شَاءَ عَذَّبَهُ.

records.chapter: Reward of offering five times’ prayers on prime time
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2. And from the previous chain from al-Hussain b. Sa’d from Ibn Abi ’Umayr from Ismail al-Basri from al-Fadhl from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Messenger of Allah (sw) entered the mosque where his companions were gathered. He questioned them, 'Are you familiar with what your Lord has stated?' They replied, 'Allah and His Messenger know best.' He then said, 'Verily, your Lord has stated: 'One who offers obligatory prayers five times and that too on prime time, he will come to Me with an assurance on the Day of Judgment and I would make him enter Paradise. And for a person who did not offer prayers on time, I have choice whether to punish him or forgive him’.”'
records.arabicText: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَعْدٍ عَنِ ابْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ إِسْمَاعِیلَ الْبَصْرِیِّ عَنِ الْفَضْلِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: دَخَلَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص الْمَسْجِدَ وَ فِیهِ نَاسٌ مِنْ أَصْحَابِهِ قَالَ تَدْرُونَ مَا قَالَ رَبُّکُمْ قَالُوا اللَّهُ وَ رَسُولُهُ أَعْلَمُ قَالَ إِنَّ رَبَّکُمْ یَقُولُ هَذِهِ الصَّلَوَاتُ الْخَمْسُ الْمَفْرُوضَاتُ فَمَنْ صَلَّاهُنَّ لِوَقْتِهِنَّ وَ حَافَظَ عَلَیْهِنَّ لَقِیَنِی یَوْمَ الْقِیَامَهِ وَ لَهُ عِنْدِی عَهْدٌ أُدْخِلُهُ بِهِ الْجَنَّهَ وَ مَنْ لَمْ یُصَلِّهِنَّ لِوَقْتِهِنَّ وَ لَمْ یُحَافِظْ عَلَیْهِنَّ فَذَلِکَ إِلَیَّ إِنْ شِئْتُ عَذَّبْتُهُ وَ إِنْ شِئْتُ غَفَرْتُ لَهُ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2. And from the previous chain from al-Hussain b. Sa’d from Ibn Abi ’Umayr from Ismail al-Basri from al-Fadhl from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Messenger of Allah (sw) entered the mosque where his companions were gathered. He questioned them, 'Are you familiar with what your Lord has stated?' They replied, 'Allah and His Messenger know best.' He then said, 'Verily, your Lord has stated: 'One who offers obligatory prayers five times and that too on prime time, he will come to Me with an assurance on the Day of Judgment and I would make him enter Paradise. And for a person who did not offer prayers on time, I have choice whether to punish him or forgive him’.”'

records.chapter: Reward of Nafila prayers
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from al-Hassan al-Wasiti al-Nahhas from Musa b. Bakr from Abi al-Hassan (a.s.) who said: “Nafila prayers are always near to a believer.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ الْوَاسِطِیِّ النَّحَّاسِ عَنْ مُوسَی بْنِ بَکْرٍ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ ع قَالَ: صَلَاهُ النَّوَافِلِ قُرْبَانُ کُلِّ مُؤْمِنٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Abi Abdillah from al-Hassan b. Mahbub from al-Hassan al-Wasiti al-Nahhas from Musa b. Bakr from Abi al-Hassan (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Nafila prayers are always near to a believer.”

records.chapter: Reward of lighting a lamp in the mosque
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) says, “One who lights a lamp in the  mosque of Allah the  people  of heaven and Allah’s angels seek repentance for that person till the time that lamp is  on in the mosque.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ ره عَنْ عَمِّهِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَبِی مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ الصَّیْرَفِیِّ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ شکر [یَشْکُرَ] الْبَاهِلِیِّ عَنِ الْکَاهِلِیِّ عَنْ أَنَسٍ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَنْ أَسْرَجَ فِی مَسْجِدٍ مِنْ مَسَاجِدِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ سِرَاجاً لَمْ تَزَلِ الْمَلَائِکَهُ وَ حَمَلَهُ الْعَرْشِ یَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لَهُ مَا دَامَ فِی ذَلِکَ الْمَسْجِدِ ضَوْءٌ مِنَ السِّرَاجِ.

records.chapter: Reward of retaining spit in the mouth during Salaat
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) from Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Ali b. Ahmad from Sahl b. Darihi from his father from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “One who retains his saliva during Salat out of respect of Allah’s Majesty and His Honor, Allah keeps him healthy till his death.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ سَعْدِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ حَسَّانَ عَنْ سَهْلِ بْنِ دَارَهَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ حَبَسَ رِیقَهُ إِجْلَالًا لِلَّهِ تَعَالَی فِی صَلَاتِهِ أَوْرَثَهُ اللَّهُ صِحَّهً حَتَّی الْمَمَاتِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) from Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Ali b. Ahmad from Sahl b. Darihi from his father from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “One who retains his saliva during Salat out of respect of Allah’s Majesty and His Honor, Allah keeps him healthy till his death.”

records.chapter: Reward of offering prayer in Masjidul Haraam
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ridha (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said, “The reward of a Salaat offered in Masjidul Haraam is more than the reward of a hundred thousand prayers offered in some other mosque.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَعْبَدٍ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ خَالِدٍ عَنْ أَبِی الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ الْبَاقِرُ ع صَلَاهٌ فِی الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ أَفْضَلُ مِنْ مِائَهِ أَلْفِ صَلَاهٍ فِی غَیْرِهِ مِنَ الْمَسَاجِدِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam Ridha (a.s.) narrates from his predecessors that the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “The reward of a Salaat offered in Masjidul Haraam is more than the reward of a hundred thousand prayers offered in some other mosque.”

records.chapter: Reward of offering Prayer in Masjidun Nabawi
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his ancestors that the Holy Prophet (sw) said, “The reward of offering a Prayer  in  Masjidun  Nabawi  is  equal  to  the  reward  of  ten thousand  Prayers  offered in  any  other  mosque  except  the Prayer offered in Masjidul Haraam whose reward is equal to the reward of a hundred thousand Prayers offered in another mosque.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ جَعْفَرٍ عَنْ هَارُونَ بْنِ مُسْلِمٍ عَنْ مَسْعَدَهَ بْنِ صَدَقَهَ عَنِ الصَّادِقِ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص صَلَاهٌ فِی مَسْجِدِی تَعْدِلُ عِنْدَ اللَّهِ عَشَرَهَ آلَافِ صَلَاهٍ فِی غَیْرِهِ مِنَ الْمَسَاجِدِ إِلَّا الْمَسْجِدَ الْحَرَامَ فَإِنَّ الصَّلَاهَ فِیهِ تَعْدِلُ مِائَهَ أَلْفِ صَلَاهٍ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his ancestors that the Holy Prophet (sw) said,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “The reward of offering a Prayer  in  Masjidun  Nabawi  is  equal  to  the  reward  of  ten thousand  Prayers  offered in  any  other  mosque  except  the Prayer offered in Masjidul Haraam whose reward is equal to the reward of a hundred thousand Prayers offered in another mosque.”

records.chapter: Reward of offering Prayer between Masjidul Haraam and Masjidun Nabawi
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Hasan bin Ali Washshaa says that he asked Imam Ridha (a.s.), “Is the reward of offering Prayer in Masjidun Nabawi and  Masjidul  Haraam  equal?  He  replied,  “Yes,  and  the prayers  recited  between  them  is  equal  to 2.000 prayers recited elsewhere.”
records.arabicText: arabicnotfound
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1.  Hasan bin Ali Washshaa says that he asked Imam Ridha (a.s.),
records.thaqalaynMatn: “Is the reward of offering Prayer in Masjidun Nabawi and  Masjidul  Haraam  equal?  He  replied,  “Yes,  and  the prayers  recited  between  them  is  equal  to 2.000 prayers recited elsewhere.”

records.chapter: Reward of offering Prayer in Masjid-e-Kufa
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1.  Abu  Baseer  says  that  he  heard  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.) saying, “Masjid-e-Kufa  is  a  real mosque  for  one thousand prophets and one thousand Wasis have offered Salaat in this mosque. During the time of Nuh (a.s.), water started coming out from this place and his Ark was built over here only. Its right side possesses the pleasure of Allah. Its middle has a garden of Paradise and its left has Makr.” He asked, “What do you mean by Makr?” Imam (a.s.) replied, “It implies the buildings of Shaitaan.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ أَبِی حَمْزَهَ عَنْ أَبِی بَصِیرٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ یَقُولُ نِعْمَ الْمَسْجِدُ مَسْجِدُ الْکُوفَهِ صَلَّی فِیهِ أَلْفُ نَبِیٍّ وَ أَلْفُ وَصِیٍّ وَ مِنْهُ فارَ التَّنُّورُ وَ فِیهِ نُجِرَتِ السَّفِینَهُ مَیْمَنَتُهُ رِضْوَانُ اللَّهِ وَ وَسَطُهُ رَوْضَهٌ مِنْ رِیَاضِ الْجَنَّهِ وَ مَیْسَرَتُهُ مَکْرٌ. فَقُلْتُ لِأَبِی مَا الْمَعْنِیُّ بِقَوْلِهِ قَالَ یَعْنِی مَنَازِلَ الشَّیْطَانِ

records.chapter: Reward of offering Prayer in Masjid-e-Kufa
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 2.   Muhammad  bin  Sinaan  says  that  he  heard  Imam  Ridha (a.s.) say, “The reward of offering a prayer in Masjid-e-Kufa alone is better than the reward of offering seventy prayers in congregation in other mosques.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ سَعِیدٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا الْحَسَنِ الرِّضَا ع یَقُولُ الصَّلَاهُ فِی مَسْجِدِ الْکُوفَهِ فَرْداً أَفْضَلُ مِنْ سَبْعِینَ صَلَاهً فِی غَیْرِهِ جَمَاعَهً.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 2.   Muhammad  bin  Sinaan  says  that  he  heard  Imam  Ridha (a.s.) say,
records.thaqalaynMatn: “The reward of offering a prayer in Masjid-e-Kufa alone is better than the reward of offering seventy prayers in congregation in other mosques.”

records.chapter: Reward of offering Prayer in Masjid-e-Kufa
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 3. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh (rah) who said it was narrated to me by my uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi from his father from Muhammad b. Sinan from al-Muffadhal b. Umar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “A single prayer of Masjid-e- Kufa is equal to one thousand prayers of other mosques.”
records.arabicText: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَمِّی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ أَبِی الْقَاسِمِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ الْبَرْقِیِّ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنِ الْمُفَضَّلِ بْنِ عُمَرَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: صَلَاهٌ فِی مَسْجِدِ الْکُوفَهِ تَعْدِلُ أَلْفَ صَلَاهٍ فِی غَیْرِهِ مِنَ الْمَسَاجِدِ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 3. It was narrated to me by Muhammad b. Ali b. Majluwayh (rah) who said it was narrated to me by my uncle Muhammad b. Abi al-Qasim from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Abi Abdillah al-Barqi from his father from Muhammad b. Sinan from al-Muffadhal b. Umar from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “A single prayer of Masjid-e- Kufa is equal to one thousand prayers of other mosques.”

records.chapter: Reward of offering Salaat in Baitul Muqaddas, Masjid-e-Azam, Masjid-e-Qabeelah and Masjid-e-Sooq
records.author: Shaykh Muḥammad b. ʿAlī al-Ṣaduq
records.translator: Sayed Athar Husain Rizvi & Sayed Maqsood Athar
records.englishText: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Ahmad al-Razi from al-Nawfali from al-Sakuni from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his forefathers from Ali (a.s.) who said: “A single Salaat of Baitul Muqaddas is equal to one thousand prayers. A single prayer of Masjid-e-Azam is equal to one hundred prayers. A single prayer of Masjid-e-Qabeelah is equal to twenty-five prayers and a single prayer of Masjid-e-Sooq is equal to twelve prayers. A Salaat offered in one’s house is equal to a single prayer only.”
records.arabicText: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ إِدْرِیسَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَحْمَدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَسَّانَ الرَّازِیِّ عَنِ النَّوْفَلِیِّ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ عَنْ آبَائِهِ عَنْ عَلِیٍّ ع قَالَ: صَلَاهٌ فِی الْبَیْتِ الْمُقَدَّسِ تَعْدِلُ أَلْفَ صَلَاهٍ وَ صَلَاهٌ فِی مَسْجِدِ الْأَعْظَمِ مِائَهُ صَلَاهٍ وَ صَلَاهٌ فِی مَسْجِدِ الْقَبِیلَهِ خَمْسٌ وَ عِشْرُونَ صَلَاهً وَ صَلَاهٌ فِی مَسْجِدِ السُّوقِ اثْنَتَیْ عَشْرَهَ صَلَاهً وَ صَلَاهُ الرَّجُلِ فِی بَیْتِهِ وَحْدَهُ صَلَاهٌ وَاحِدَهٌ.
records.thaqalaynSanad: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Ahmad b. Idris from Muhammad b. Ahmad from Muhammad b. Ahmad al-Razi from al-Nawfali from al-Sakuni from Ja’far b. Muhammad from his forefathers from Ali (a.s.) who said:
records.thaqalaynMatn: “A single Salaat of Baitul Muqaddas is equal to one thousand prayers. A single prayer of Masjid-e-Azam is equal to one hundred prayers. A single prayer of Masjid-e-Qabeelah is equal to twenty-five prayers and a single prayer of Masjid-e-Sooq is equal to twelve prayers. A Salaat offered in one’s house is equal to a single prayer only.”
